danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy mech designs and projectiles from the five hostile worlds
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring pilot evasion and combat response

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual threshold crossing into each of the five Aki-Do worlds
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating danger zones; progression into enemy territory

danger_signals.visual_form: MBS (Morphing Battle System) visual degradation or status indicators
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat to pilot survival; need for evasive maneuvers or mode switching

safety_signals.visual_form: Four distinct mech mode transformations: Walker, Sonic Jet, Land Burner, Hard Shell
safety_signals.meaning: Player agency and tactical adaptation; mode-specific defensive/offensive postures

safety_signals.visual_form: Visual targeting or waypoint markers toward the Deoberon core
safety_signals.meaning: Goal-oriented progression; clear mission endpoint reduces ambient dread

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Five distinct hostile worlds with unique enemy designs and environmental aesthetics
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Five separate world domains within the Vortex; each world visually distinct to signal faction territory
The Deoberon core location; implicitly friendly/allied space being invaded

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Aki-Do Forces occupy and defend five distinct worlds; hostile environment aesthetics
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies actively pursue and attack the player's MBS; defensive formations and attack patterns escalate per world

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Vortex entry transitions; each world level presents escalating visual complexity and 3D environmental design
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy density, attack sophistication, and world hazards increase as player approaches core retrieval objective

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Real-time mech mode transformations (Walker/Sonic Jet/Land Burner/Hard Shell) visually alter silhouette and capabilities
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can adapt movement speed, defensive posture, or offensive stance by switching modes; world geometry accommodates multiple traversal styles

environmental_storytelling.example: Five distinct Aki-Do worlds convey invasion depth; each world's visual theme (3D geometry, enemy aesthetic, environmental hazards) signals a different operational theater within the hostile Vortex
The Deoberon core serves as implicit story landmark; retrieving it from the five worlds frames the conflict as resource extraction under enemy fire

The mech's ability to shift between four modes (Walker/Jet/Burner/Shell) reflects the 'Morphing Battle System' concept; visual flexibility mirrors the player's tactical options against diverse enemy types

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Active pursuit, organized attack patterns, escalating difficulty per world; enemies coordinate zone control
Transformation between modes signals tactical adaptation; slower Walker mode for complex terrain, Sonic Jet for speed, Land Burner for ground assault, Hard Shell for maximum defense
3D geometry acts as obstacle and hazard; world design shifts per level to signal changing tactical conditions

notes: Vortex is a 3D shooter with limited narrative exposition in available sources. World-state communication relies primarily on mechanical transformation (mode-switching), level progression through five enemy worlds, and the core retrieval objective. Danger is conveyed through enemy presence and world escalation rather than explicit visual storytelling (e.g., UI overlays or cinematic cues). The paradigm is shmup with 3D Super FX graphics; manifestation is largely spatial (environment design, enemy placement, mech silhouette changes) rather than color-coded or symbolic.
